framework.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

sections.section_title: Introduction: Protecting Cardholder Data with PCI Security Standards

policies.policy_title: Protecting Cardholder Data with PCI Security Standards
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the procedures and technologies to be used by merchants to protect cardholder data and prevent financial fraud. It is essential to comply with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to alleviate vulnerabilities and safeguard cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: All merchants must adhere to the PCI DSS to ensure the security of cardholder data. This includes implementing standard security procedures and technologies to prevent theft of cardholder data.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all merchants, service providers, and acquirers involved in payment card transactions. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for card-processing, including point-of-sale devices, mobile devices, personal computers or servers, wireless hotspots, web shopping applications, paper-based storage systems, and remote access connections.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to protect cardholder data from theft and unauthorized access. This is achieved by implementing the PCI DSS and ensuring that all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for card-processing are secure and compliant.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for implementing the PCI DSS, including the use of standard security procedures and technologies to prevent theft of cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Implement the PCI DSS requirements, including the use of standard security procedures and technologies to prevent theft of cardholder data.
control.WHO: All merchants, service providers, and acquirers involved in payment card transactions.
control.WHEN: Immediately, with regular reviews and updates to ensure compliance.
control.HOW: By conducting regular risk assessments, implementing security measures, and monitoring systems for vulnerabilities.
control.WHERE: All systems, applications, and infrastructure used for card-processing.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Implement PCI DSS Requirements for Cardholder Data
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement the PCI DSS requirements, including the use of standard security procedures and technologies to prevent theft of cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems, applications, and infrastructure used for card-processing.
compliances.Objective: To ensure the security of cardholder data and prevent unauthorized access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in financial loss and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement a robust access control system to restrict access to cardholder data.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments to identify and address potential security risks.
mitigation.3: Implement encryption and secure transmission protocols to protect cardholder data in transit.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data due to weak security controls or lack of encryption.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in financial loss and reputational damage.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data due to weak security controls or lack of encryption.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a robust access control system to restrict access to cardholder data.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments to identify and address potential security risks.
RiskMitigation.3: Implement encryption and secure transmission protocols to protect cardholder data in transit.

RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data and ensure compliance.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the protection of cardholder data and maintain PCI DSS compliance through ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
control.WHO: Information Security Team
control.WHEN: Quarterly
control.HOW: Utilize industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools and penetration testing methodologies.
control.WHERE: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses in systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: Identify and mitigate potential security weaknesses in systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, financial losses, damage to reputation.

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify, prioritize, and remediate vulnerabilities.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
mitigation.3: Implement a patch management program to ensure timely installation of security patches.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, financial losses, damage to reputation.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, financial losses, damage to reputation.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can result in financial losses, damage to reputation, and loss of customer trust.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify, prioritize, and remediate vulnerabilities.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
RiskMitigation.3: Implement a patch management program to ensure timely installation of security patches.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect payment card transaction environments and ensure the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must comply with the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data, and to maintain compliance with the PCI DSS.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
subpolicies.control: WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing; WHO: Information Security Team; WHEN: Quarterly; HOW: Utilize industry-recognized tools and methodologies; WHERE: All payment card processing environments.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Perform quarterly vulnerability assessments and penetration testing on all payment card processing environments to identify potential security risks.
compliances.Scope: All payment card processing environments
compliances.Objective: Identify and mitigate potential security risks to protect cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security awareness training for employees to prevent social engineering attacks.
mitigation.3: Implement a web application firewall to protect against common web attacks.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can result in data breaches, financial losses, and damage to the organization's reputation.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular security awareness training for employees to prevent social engineering attacks.
RiskMitigation.3: Implement a web application firewall to protect against common web attacks.

compliances.Objective: Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements by maintaining an accurate inventory of IT assets and business processes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance with regulatory requirements, fines, and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement an IT asset management system to track and update IT assets and business processes.

sections.section_title: Security Controls and Processes for PCI DSS Requirements

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance, ensuring the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must comply with the PCI DSS. This policy outlines the steps to be taken to ensure compliance, including assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data, and to maintain PCI DSS compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Assessment and Inventory of Cardholder Data
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular risk assessments and inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
control.WHO: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
control.WHEN: Quarterly, with additional assessments as needed.
control.HOW: Use a risk assessment framework and inventory tools to identify vulnerabilities and exposures.
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Regular Risk Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team must conduct regular risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.
compliances.Objective: To identify and mitigate potential risks to cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement encryption and access controls to protect cardholder data.
mitigation.2: Regularly update and patch software and systems to prevent vulnerabilities.
mitigation.3: Conduct regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.
compliances.SubPolicyName: Assessment and Inventory of Cardholder Data

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can result in data breaches, financial losses, and damage to reputation.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement encryption and access controls to protect cardholder data.
RiskMitigation.2: Regularly update and patch software and systems to prevent vulnerabilities.
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments.

subpolicy_info.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular risk assessments and inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
control.WHO: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
control.WHEN: Quarterly, with additional assessments as needed.
control.HOW: Use a risk assessment framework and inventory tools to identify vulnerabilities and exposures.
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fix identified vulnerabilities, securely remove any unnecessary cardholder data storage, and implement secure business processes.

control.WHAT: Implement security patches, updates, and configuration changes to address identified vulnerabilities.
control.WHO: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
control.WHEN: Immediately, with additional remediation as needed.
control.HOW: Use a vulnerability management framework and remediation tools to address identified vulnerabilities.
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Implement Security Patches and Updates
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly apply security patches and updates to address identified vulnerabilities in systems and applications.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.
compliances.Objective: Ensure the security and integrity of cardholder data by addressing identified vulnerabilities.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management framework to identify and prioritize vulnerabilities.
mitigation.2: Use automated tools to apply security patches and updates.
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to ensure vulnerabilities are addressed.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unpatched vulnerabilities can be exploited by attackers, leading to data breaches and reputational damage.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and reputational damage.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unpatched vulnerabilities in systems and applications can be exploited by attackers, leading to unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and reputational damage.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management framework to identify and prioritize vulnerabilities.
RiskMitigation.2: Use automated tools to apply security patches and updates.
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to ensure vulnerabilities are addressed.

sections.section_title: Build and Maintain a Secure Network and Systems

policies.policy_title: Build and Maintain a Secure Network and Systems
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for building and maintaining a secure network and systems to protect cardholder data and sensitive authentication data. It follows the PCI DSS guidelines and ensures compliance with security best practices.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to this policy. The policy is divided into three ongoing steps: Assess, Repair, and Report.
policies.scope: All organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. This policy applies to all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment card processing, including public networks, wireless networks, and internet connections.
policies.objective: To ensure the security of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data by implementing the PCI DSS guidelines and security best practices. The primary goals of this policy are to identify and remediate vulnerabilities, securely remove unnecessary cardholder data storage, and implement secure business processes.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data and take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
subpolicies.control: WHAT: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify all locations of cardholder data. WHO: The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and the IT department. WHEN: Quarterly. HOW: Use a risk assessment framework to identify vulnerabilities and prioritize remediation efforts. WHERE: All systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment card processing.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Quarterly Risk Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and the IT department must conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify all locations of cardholder data on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Scope: All systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities and prioritize remediation efforts
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Department, Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data, and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement a risk assessment framework to identify vulnerabilities and prioritize remediation efforts.
mitigation.2: Provide regular training to the IT department and CISO on risk assessment and remediation best practices.
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with the risk assessment and remediation plan.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data, reputational damage.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Department, Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data, reputational damage.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: IT Department, Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
risk_details.RiskDescription: Non-compliance with quarterly risk assessments may lead to data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data, and reputational damage.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a risk assessment framework to identify vulnerabilities and prioritize remediation efforts.
RiskMitigation.2: Provide regular training to the IT department and CISO on risk assessment and remediation best practices.
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with the risk assessment and remediation plan.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Remediate Identified Vulnerabilities
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All identified vulnerabilities must be remediated within 24 hours of identification to prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure the security of cardholder data by remedying identified vulnerabilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, leading to data breaches and potential financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities on a regular basis
mitigation.2: Provide regular security awareness training to IT and development teams on secure coding practices and vulnerability remediation
mitigation.3: Use automated vulnerability scanning tools to identify and remediate vulnerabilities

risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can occur due to unremediated vulnerabilities, leading to data breaches and potential financial losses

RiskMitigation.2: Provide regular security awareness training to IT and development teams on secure coding practices and vulnerability remediation
RiskMitigation.3: Use automated vulnerability scanning tools to identify and remediate vulnerabilities

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the procedures for protecting cardholder data in accordance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). It ensures the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data, and adheres to the Assess, Repair, and Report steps outlined in the PCI DSS.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. The PCI DSS is a continuous process that involves ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment card processing, including POS systems, internet networks, wireless networks, and public networks.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data, and to adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities, securely removing unnecessary cardholder data storage, and implementing secure business processes.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for maintaining a vulnerability management program to protect cardholder data and ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards.
policies.policy_text: The organization shall maintain a vulnerability management program to identify, assess, and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner. The program shall include ongoing steps for adhering to PCI DSS standards, including assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data and maintain compliance with PCI DSS standards by identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in a timely manner.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and assessing vulnerabilities in the organization's systems and processes.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments using approved tools and methodologies.
control.WHO: The Information Security team shall be responsible for conducting vulnerability assessments.
control.WHEN: Vulnerability assessments shall be conducted at least quarterly, with additional assessments as needed.
control.HOW: Vulnerability assessments shall be conducted using approved tools and methodologies, and results shall be documented and reported to management.
control.WHERE: Vulnerability assessments shall be conducted on all systems and processes that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The Information Security team shall conduct regular vulnerability assessments using approved tools and methodologies on all systems and processes that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data
compliances.Objective: To identify and assess vulnerabilities in the organization's systems and processes to prevent data breaches and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner
mitigation.2: Provide regular training to employees on vulnerability assessment and remediation best practices
mitigation.3: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities in systems and processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, and reputational damage

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, and reputational damage
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to sensitive data can occur due to vulnerabilities in systems and processes, leading to data breaches and reputational damage

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner
RiskMitigation.2: Provide regular training to employees on vulnerability assessment and remediation best practices
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities in systems and processes

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for implementing strong access control measures to protect sensitive information and ensure compliance with relevant security standards.
policies.policy_text: All personnel with access to sensitive information must adhere to the following requirements: (1) Use strong, unique passwords for all systems and applications; (2) Implement multi-factor authentication for all remote access; (3) Limit access to sensitive information based on job function and need-to-know; (4) Regularly review and update access controls to ensure they remain effective.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all personnel with access to sensitive information, including employees, contractors, and third-party vendors. It applies to all systems, applications, and infrastructure that store, process, or transmit sensitive information.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all personnel with access to sensitive information adhere to strong access control measures to protect sensitive information and ensure compliance with relevant security standards.

mitigation.3: Provide password management training to personnel

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems

RiskMitigation.3: Provide password management training to personnel

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for regularly monitoring and testing networks to ensure the security and integrity of cardholder data. It is a critical component of the PCI DSS compliance process.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must regularly monitor and test their networks to ensure the security and integrity of cardholder data. This includes identifying vulnerabilities, fixing identified vulnerabilities, and implementing secure business processes.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the security and integrity of cardholder data by regularly monitoring and testing networks, identifying vulnerabilities, and implementing secure business processes.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for maintaining an information security policy in accordance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). The policy aims to protect cardholder data and sensitive authentication data by adhering to the PCI DSS guidelines.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS guidelines. The PCI DSS compliance is a continuous process that involves ongoing steps of Assess, Repair, and Report.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data by adhering to the PCI DSS guidelines, thereby reducing the risk of data breaches and maintaining the trust of customers.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying all locations of cardholder data, taking an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyzing them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHEN: On a quarterly basis, or as needed
control.HOW: Use a risk assessment framework to identify vulnerabilities and implement controls to mitigate risks
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted

compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Department, Security Team, and Management

mitigation.2: Conduct regular risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities and implement controls to mitigate risks.
mitigation.3: Develop and implement a data classification policy to categorize cardholder data based on its sensitivity.

sections.section_title: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements

policies.policy_title: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the compensating controls required for PCI DSS compliance, ensuring the protection of payment card transaction environments.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This policy provides supplemental information to help understand and apply the PCI DSS.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the protection of payment card transaction environments by implementing compensating controls for PCI DSS compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
control.WHO: Information Security Team
control.WHEN: Quarterly
control.HOW: Utilize industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools and penetration testing methodologies.
control.WHERE: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
compliances.Objective: To identify and remediate potential security vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify, prioritize, and remediate vulnerabilities.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to simulate attacks and identify vulnerabilities.
mitigation.3: Provide training to employees on vulnerability management and remediation best practices.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data due to unpatched vulnerabilities.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Financial losses due to data breaches.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Financial losses due to data breaches.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data due to unpatched vulnerabilities.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify, prioritize, and remediate vulnerabilities.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to simulate attacks and identify vulnerabilities.
RiskMitigation.3: Provide training to employees on vulnerability management and remediation best practices.

compliances.Objective: To identify potential security vulnerabilities using industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to identify potential security vulnerabilities, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Select and implement industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools.
mitigation.2: Configure vulnerability scanning tools to scan all systems and networks regularly.
mitigation.3: Provide training to employees on vulnerability scanning tool usage and best practices.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to identify potential security vulnerabilities due to inadequate vulnerability scanning tools.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Financial losses due to data breaches.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Failure to identify potential security vulnerabilities, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.

RiskMitigation.2: Configure vulnerability scanning tools to scan all systems and networks regularly.
RiskMitigation.3: Provide training to employees on vulnerability scanning tool usage and best practices.

subpolicy_info.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implement secure business processes for payment card processing, including secure storage, transmission, and disposal of cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Implement secure protocols for storing, transmitting, and disposing of cardholder data, such as encryption and secure deletion.
control.WHO: Information Security Team and Business Process Owners
control.WHEN: Immediately upon identification of vulnerabilities or changes to business processes.
control.HOW: Utilize industry-recognized secure protocols and best practices for data storage, transmission, and disposal.
control.WHERE: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Implement Secure Protocols for Storing Cardholder Data
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement secure protocols for storing cardholder data, such as encryption, to prevent unauthorized access.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks that store cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of cardholder data.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: Information Security Team and Business Process Owners
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, leading to financial loss, reputational damage, and regulatory fines.

mitigation.1: Implement encryption protocols, such as AES-256, for all cardholder data storage.
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update encryption protocols to ensure they remain effective against evolving threats.
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities in cardholder data storage.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data through vulnerabilities in encryption protocols or inadequate security audits.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, leading to financial loss, reputational damage, and regulatory fines.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can occur through vulnerabilities in encryption protocols or inadequate security audits.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement encryption protocols, such as AES-256, for all cardholder data storage.
RiskMitigation.2: Regularly review and update encryption protocols to ensure they remain effective against evolving threats.
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities in cardholder data storage.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data and ensure compliance with industry standards.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the protection of cardholder data and maintain PCI DSS compliance through ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for identifying all locations of cardholder data, taking an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyzing them for vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.control: WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing. WHO: Information Security Team. WHEN: Quarterly. HOW: Utilize industry-recognized tools and methodologies. WHERE: All systems and networks involved in payment card processing.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security threats and weaknesses in systems and networks involved in payment card processing.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To identify and mitigate potential security threats and weaknesses in systems and networks involved in payment card processing
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify potential security threats
mitigation.3: Implement a incident response plan to respond to security incidents

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, financial losses

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, financial losses
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to sensitive data can result in data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing to identify potential security threats
RiskMitigation.3: Implement a incident response plan to respond to security incidents

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for selecting a qualified security assessor to ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards. It covers the assessment, repair, and reporting process for entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: Entities must select a qualified security assessor to conduct regular security assessments and provide recommendations for remediation. The assessor must be approved by the PCI Security Standards Council and have experience in conducting PCI DSS assessments.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the selection of a qualified security assessor to conduct regular security assessments and provide recommendations for remediation, thereby ensuring compliance with PCI DSS standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The qualified security assessor must conduct a thorough assessment of the entity's security controls and provide recommendations for remediation.
subpolicies.control: WHAT: Conduct a thorough assessment of security controls; WHO: Qualified security assessor; WHEN: Regularly, as specified by PCI DSS; HOW: Using approved assessment methods and tools; WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The qualified security assessor must conduct a thorough assessment of the entity's security controls and provide recommendations for remediation on a regular basis as specified by PCI DSS.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted
compliances.Objective: To ensure the security controls are effective in protecting cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement a robust security awareness program for employees
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments
mitigation.3: Provide ongoing training and education to security personnel

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for selecting and managing approved scanning vendors to ensure the security and compliance of payment card transaction environments.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must select and manage approved scanning vendors to ensure the security and compliance of payment card transaction environments.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the selection and management of approved scanning vendors that meet the requirements of the PCI DSS and related security standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Approved scanning vendors must meet the following criteria: (1) have a proven track record of security and compliance, (2) have a robust security framework in place, and (3) have a clear understanding of the PCI DSS requirements.
subpolicies.control: The following controls must be implemented to ensure the selection of approved scanning vendors: (1) WHO: The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and the procurement team must work together to select approved scanning vendors. (2) WHAT: The CISO and the procurement team must evaluate the vendor's security and compliance track record, security framework, and understanding of the PCI DSS requirements. (3) WHEN: The evaluation must be conducted annually or as needed. (4) HOW: The evaluation must be based on a comprehensive review of the vendor's security and compliance documentation. (5) WHERE: The evaluation must be conducted in a secure and confidential manner.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Vendor Selection Criteria - CISO and Procurement Team Collaboration
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The CISO and procurement team must work together to select approved scanning vendors.
compliances.Scope: All vendor selection processes
compliances.Objective: Ensure collaboration between CISO and procurement team for vendor selection
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate vendor selection may lead to security and compliance risks.

mitigation.1: Establish clear communication channels between CISO and procurement team
mitigation.2: Define roles and responsibilities for vendor selection
mitigation.3: Conduct regular training sessions for CISO and procurement team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate vendor selection may lead to security and compliance risks.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Inadequate vendor selection may lead to security and compliance risks.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Inadequate vendor selection may lead to security and compliance risks, which can result in financial losses, reputational damage, and legal liabilities.

RiskMitigation.1: Establish clear communication channels between CISO and procurement team
RiskMitigation.2: Define roles and responsibilities for vendor selection
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct regular training sessions for CISO and procurement team

(3) WHEN: The evaluation must be conducted annually or as needed. (4) HOW: The evaluation must be based on a comprehensive review of the vendor's security and compliance documentation. (5) WHERE: The evaluation must be conducted in a secure and confidential manner.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the scope of PCI DSS requirements for all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS requirements. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the security of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data by adhering to the PCI DSS requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
control.WHO: IT Security Team, Management
control.WHEN: Annually, or as needed
control.HOW: Use a recognized risk assessment framework, such as NIST 800-30, to identify vulnerabilities.
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct a Thorough Risk Assessment
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a thorough risk assessment to identify vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data and ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in financial loss, reputational damage, and regulatory fines.

mitigation.1: Implement a risk management framework to identify and mitigate vulnerabilities.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities.
mitigation.3: Develop and implement a vulnerability management program to address identified vulnerabilities.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in financial loss, reputational damage, and regulatory fines.

policies.policy_title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect payment card transaction environments and ensure compliance with relevant regulations.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must comply with the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the secure storage, processing, and transmission of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data, and to maintain compliance with the PCI DSS.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for reporting compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) and related security standards. It ensures that all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data adhere to the PCI DSS and submit compliance reports to the acquiring bank and card brands.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS and submit compliance reports to the acquiring bank and card brands. The PCI DSS compliance process involves three ongoing steps: Assess, Repair, and Report.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data adhere to the PCI DSS and submit compliance reports to the acquiring bank and card brands, thereby protecting cardholder data and preventing security breaches.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing and remediating vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
subpolicies.control: WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and remediate identified vulnerabilities. WHO: Information Security Team. WHEN: Quarterly. HOW: Utilize industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools. WHERE: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Perform quarterly vulnerability assessments using industry-recognized tools to identify potential security threats.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
compliances.Objective: Identify and remediate vulnerabilities to prevent data breaches.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in financial loss and reputational damage.

mitigation.2: Provide regular training to the Information Security Team on vulnerability assessment and remediation techniques.
mitigation.3: Use industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools to ensure accurate and comprehensive assessments.

sections.section_title: Implementing PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes

policies.policy_title: Implementing PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the procedures for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) by identifying, repairing, and reporting vulnerabilities in the payment card transaction environment.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This policy outlines the procedures for identifying, repairing, and reporting vulnerabilities in the payment card transaction environment.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the continuous protection of cardholder data and sensitive authentication data by adhering to the PCI DSS and related security standards.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
control.WHO: Information Security Team
control.WHEN: Quarterly
control.HOW: Utilize industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools and penetration testing methodologies.
control.WHERE: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses in all systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and networks that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data
compliances.Objective: To identify and mitigate potential security weaknesses in systems and networks that handle cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, financial loss, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing
mitigation.3: Use industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools

RiskMitigation.3: Use industry-recognized vulnerability scanning tools

policies.policy_title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect payment card transaction environments and ensure compliance with relevant regulations.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must comply with the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. Compliance is a continuous process that involves ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the security of payment card transaction environments and maintain compliance with the PCI DSS by implementing effective security measures and controls.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular security assessments and inventory of IT assets and business processes
control.WHO: Information Security Team
control.WHEN: Quarterly
control.HOW: Utilize industry-recognized security assessment tools and methodologies
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular security assessments to identify vulnerabilities in IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted
compliances.Objective: To identify and mitigate potential security risks to cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing and risk assessments
mitigation.3: Implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system

sections.section_title: About the PCI Security Standards Council

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data and ensure compliance with industry standards.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors.
policies.objective: To ensure the protection of cardholder data and maintain PCI DSS compliance through ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, and analyze them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular risk assessments and inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
control.WHO: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
control.WHEN: Quarterly, with additional assessments as needed.
control.HOW: Use industry-recognized risk assessment tools and methodologies, such as the PCI DSS Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) and the Payment Application Data Security Standard (PA-DSS).
control.WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Conduct Regular Risk Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Perform regular risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities in IT assets and business processes for payment card processing.
compliances.Scope: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted.
compliances.Objective: To identify and mitigate potential security risks to cardholder data.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement a risk-based approach to security, focusing on high-risk areas.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing.
mitigation.3: Develop and implement a comprehensive incident response plan.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, and financial losses.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: IT Department, Management, and Compliance Team.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to cardholder data can lead to data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a risk-based approach to security, focusing on high-risk areas.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing.
RiskMitigation.3: Develop and implement a comprehensive incident response plan.

policies.policy_title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance Policy
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for adhering to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect payment card transaction environments and ensure compliance with relevant regulations.
policies.policy_text: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data must adhere to the PCI DSS. This includes merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. The PCI DSS is a continuous process that involves ongoing assessment, repair, and reporting.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment card issuing banks, processors, developers, and other vendors. The scope includes all locations, systems, and personnel involved in payment card processing.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure compliance with the PCI DSS and protect payment card transaction environments from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, modification, or destruction. The specific goals and outcomes of this policy include:
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing and remediating vulnerabilities in payment card processing environments.

control.WHAT: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses.
control.WHO: The Information Security team, in collaboration with the IT department and other relevant stakeholders.
control.WHEN: At least quarterly, with additional assessments as needed.
control.HOW: Using industry-recognized tools and methodologies, such as the PCI DSS Vulnerability Scanning Requirements.
control.WHERE: All payment card processing environments, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile systems.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Vulnerability Assessments
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify potential security weaknesses in payment card processing environments.
compliances.Scope: All payment card processing environments, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile systems.
compliances.Objective: To identify and remediate vulnerabilities in payment card processing environments to prevent data breaches and maintain compliance with PCI DSS.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: Information Security, IT, and relevant stakeholders.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive payment card data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
mitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments to identify potential security weaknesses.
mitigation.3: Provide training to IT staff on vulnerability assessment and remediation techniques.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive payment card data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Financial losses and damage to reputation.

risk_details.RiskTitle: Unauthorized Access to Sensitive Payment Card Data
risk_details.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive payment card data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Financial losses and damage to reputation.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unauthorized access to sensitive payment card data can result in data breaches and financial losses.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
RiskMitigation.2: Conduct regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments to identify potential security weaknesses.
RiskMitigation.3: Provide training to IT staff on vulnerability assessment and remediation techniques.
